description_long: This is a tall, rectangular antique bottle with a long cylindrical neck and a narrow mouth. The body is dark and weathered, showing signs of oxidation and patina, suggesting it is made of metal, likely brass or copper. The front face features intricate embossed designs and text, possibly indicating branding or product information, while the back is plain except for a small, faded label. The bottle has a hexagonal cross-section, giving it a faceted appearance along the sides. The surface is rough and uneven, with visible discoloration and patches of corrosion. The overall look is aged and utilitarian, reminiscent of old medicinal or apothecary bottles found in urban historical settings.
description: A tall, antique metal bottle with a rectangular body, long neck, embossed front, and weathered, corroded surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing embossed designs and text on the main face, with a clear view of the bottle's rectangular body and cylindrical neck.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the hexagonal, faceted shape of the bottle and the narrowness of the profile.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain, weathered surface with a small, faded label affixed to the back.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the faceted, hexagonal profile and the bottle's aged, corroded surface.
attribute_label_or_branding: Embossed text and decorative elements on the front face, with a small faded paper label on the back; details are difficult to read due to wear.
attribute_damage_or_deformation: Visible discoloration, corrosion, and surface wear; no major dents or cracks.
